title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Respect, and Product Contract · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds inclusive feed-reader product contract from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of inclusive feed-reader product contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value.
Specify respectful identification, fetch cadence, caching, correction, deletion, source pause, and incident contact behavior.
Separate source discovery, ingestion, storage, scheduling, API, reader state, and presentation with explicit accepted unknowns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Reader Job
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9549 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate reader, publisher, inclusive, privacy, and changed-case product outcomes; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Reader Job.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Reader Job, keep Feed Ts Reader Job connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Feed Ts Reader Job preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
More aggregated posts automatically create more reader value. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
